resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def longestSemiRepetitiveSubstring(self, s: str) -> int:
        n = len(s)
        res = 0
        for i in range(n):
            count = 1
            for j in range(i + 1, n):
                if s[j] == s[j - 1]:
                    count += 1
                else:
                    count = 1
                res = max(res, count)
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a two-pointer approach to iterate through the string and count the consecutive occurrences of the same digit. It keeps track of the maximum count seen so far and returns it as the result. The time complexity is O(n^2) due to the nested loops, but the space complexity is O(1) as it only uses a constant amount of space.
